methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Valthos, a Defense & Security seed-stage company founded by Kathleen McMahon and Tess van Stekelenburg, has raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector at seed and adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $30M raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $75M valuation. The peer-median method references four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Tenna Systems at $60M (seed), and three Series A companies—Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Stark Defence ($500M)—yielding a median of $225M. The peer range spans $60M to $500M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $64M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $150M. The $64M floor reflects conservatism from stage-progression alone; the $259M ceiling is weighted toward peer comparables. Key uncertainties center on stage discount: Valthos is earlier than most comparables, and the degree to which that discount applies materially affects whether the company clusters toward the lower or median portion of the range. Revenue traction, product maturity, and competitive positioning within the Defense & Security subset would refine this estimate.
